what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard request item that charters the capability-workshop channel, defining its purpose, scope, core principles, expected preparation outputs, Tim-facing role, and decision boundaries before any build work begins.
observations: It is filed as a request-type noticeboard item with state open by author_session chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking
The channel is explicitly a preparation lane, not a build-order lane
It names multiple participants—Codex, Workspace Agents, ChatGPT, Claude Code, Company/RHM, local/cloud models—without crowning any single one
The core principle demands a self-describing system where every platform/tool/model/agent has a discoverable capability record
Tim is positioned as semantic authority/originator rather than developer

It lists concrete preparation outputs: requirements packs, platform/resource packs, evidence-backed possibility records, registry/capability schema needs, interface/workshop requirements, reversible experiments, and Tim-altitude questions
It sets a boundary: do not ask Tim to resolve implementation mechanics that agents can inspect or prototype
standouts: Self-describing system with discoverable capability records
Tim as semantic authority/originator, not developer
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, timestamps, and history
Markdown body with hierarchical headings and bullet lists
Links array is present but empty
concerns: Created and updated timestamps are identical despite the item being open, suggesting no follow-up edits yet
The author_session identifier 'chatgpt-gpt-5.5-thinking' is nonstandard and its meaning is not defined in the file
Links array is empty even though a charter might naturally reference related channels or documents
notes: A charter request that sets the capability-workshop channel's pre-build research agenda and its rules of engagement with Tim.
questions: What exact criteria promote an item from this preparation channel into a build order?
Who is responsible for synthesizing preparation outputs and making promotion decisions?
What schema or format will capability records follow?
How does this channel relate to existing build-order lanes or other noticeboard channels?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a charter document that defines preparation scope, principles, and boundaries.
apparent_themes: Distributed agent/model ecosystem rather than single primary coder
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a structured, evidence-gathering preparation space so that platform, tool, model, and interface choices can be made visible and judged by Tim at a semantic/product level before any irreversible build commitments.
novelty: It treats a channel charter as a first-class ledger request item and explicitly codifies what kinds of questions are inappropriate for the human semantic authority.
contribution: A reusable charter template for a preparation channel, including purpose, scope, expected outputs, and a boundary rule that protects Tim-facing decisions from implementation mechanics.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a noticeboard request item that charters the capability-workshop channel, defining its pre-build purpose, multi-agent/model scope, self-describing-system principle, Tim-facing semantic-authority role, expected outputs, and decision boundaries.
intention_for_embedding: It was filed to establish a preparation space for capability and platform research so that build orders only emerge after evidence-backed requirements and intent are clarified.
